Lake Rathetear
Quest Name
Totemic Breastplate


Uploaded February 7th, 2024 by iventheassassin
Send a correction
Post Comment
Banded
QuoteReply
# Sep 20 2004 at 3:24 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1672013818933
Scholar
111 posts
Banded is a nice newbie set of armor. There are thirteen (13) pieces in the set (Belt, Boots, Bracer [2], Cloak, Gauntlets, Gorget, Helm, Leggings, Mail, Mantle, Mask, Sleeves). The whole set of armor will give you +91 to your armor class (thats with 2 bracers). When I first started EQ banded was really popular and would sell for 2pp per ac point. This was pre-bazaar. Now you can get various piece in the bazaar for fairly cheap from those trying to up their blacksmithing skills.
▲ Page top
Aspiring Smiths Take Heart
QuoteReply
# Jun 30 2003 at 1:29 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591789874182
9 posts
I write this post to encourage any new blacksmiths. To my surprise I was able to complete a full suit of banded armor - including the mail and leggings - with approximately 80 points in smithing. Even at this level there were few failures on the combines. So if you're forging a full suit for one of your characters and aren't just trying to skill up, you might want to give it a try at this skill range.

Edited, Mon Jun 30 13:54:29 2003
▲ Page top
RE: Aspiring Smiths Take Heart
QuoteReply

__DEL__1591789874182

67 posts
I've have on my level 20 gnome warrior, ringmail, leather, banded mail, and acouple fine plate mail pieces, and I'm making it just fine. My gnome enchanter on the other hand id totally twinked at only level 9, and is probably doing about as good as you warrior is, I mean how many level 9 enchanters do you see running around with a Robe of the Kedge on?

# Mar 22 2002 at 5:50 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
Lately I've been twinking the poor warrior more than levelling. He's now got full crafted, and a centi longsword/shortsword combo, hero bracer, etc. I've managed to occasionally pop on and level him up to about 22. I really didn't intend to twink him quite this much, but I get a lot of trades for stuff I sell.

I've have on my level 20 gnome warrior, ringmail, leather, banded mial, and acouple fine plate mail pieces, and I'm making it just fine. My gnome enchanter on the other hand id totally twinked at only level 9, and is probably doing about as good as you warrior is, I mean how many level 9 enchanters do you see running around with a Robe of the Kedge on?
▲ Page top
Post about defensive skills
QuoteReply
# Nov 20 2001 at 7:18 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
I threw a set of banded on my new warrior at level one, along with a blackened alloy longsword (at lvl 5). At level 17, with better equipment now (blackened alloy armor, orc fangs, jagged bands, etc) His offense, defense, 1hs, 1hb, dual wield, dual attack are all maxxed. Unfortunately I let the 2hs and 2hb lag a bit :( But my point is, my skills did not suffer from the twinking. In fact i find that I max all of the skills out within 1 yellow of a new level. Nice theory, but my experience has been that it's not true. BTW, I fought mostly yellows, whites and reds through about lvl 8. Perhaps that is why the skills still went up.
▲ Page top
Out od work smith
QuoteReply
# Sep 30 2001 at 11:32 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592250056329
71 posts
I'm on the Veeshan server currenty spending obcene ammounts of plat skilling up in smithing and fletching. If anyone wants me to make them somthing send me a note at "darkestchilde@yahoo.com". Right now I have a 160 Fletching and 130 smithing but that is subject to change. If you see me on 'Skavor' I'll be glad to make the items for you if you buy the materials... for a fee of course...

#REDACTED, Posted: Sep 07 2001 at 10:02 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) E-mail me at virgocious78@hotmail.com or look for Kalbin Lvl9 Ranger HEF in Gfay/BB mts./CB zones !
#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 11 2001 at 8:56 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Definatly if i see anyone selling banded armour on the Morrel Thule server make sure u find me.
selling
QuoteReply
# Jul 31 2001 at 12:17 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591761254490
Scholar
179 posts
I make it on the Tunare Server.

Built up my skill in Freeport but it doesn't sell too good there. Most probably because of competition with other smiths and that fact that area is a hot spot for generous high lev characters giving away better armour or selling better armour for way below cost.

When I want to sell I go to the Qeynos/Everfrost area and it sells like crazy. All the equipment to make banded is there except small molds (Sorry little guys) and there are plenty of unused forges around Q.

1pp per ac is the standard price, unfortunately. For the price of materials it should be higher (About a 50% profit margin if I'm "completely" succesful the first time) But if it sold for any higher, nobody would buy it.
▲ Page top
Level 1???
QuoteReply
# Jul 06 2001 at 12:14 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
The only level 1 character that could get this is a twink, unless someone is feeling generous. Besides, you're not really helping yourself, because your defense skills will suffer, and you'll notice it in a few levels.

Banded armor cannot be dyed - only Fine Steel Plate.
▲ Page top
RE: Dye
QuoteReply
# Jan 18 2002 at 1:16 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
True. But if you want colored banded, you can get it (but can't smith it); get the Loda Kai armor off of the Loda Kais in Shadeweaver's Thicket. Same AC as banded, but with a nice dark blue (almost black) color.
▲ Page top
RE: Loda Kai
QuoteReply
# May 31 2002 at 10:18 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591776823129
Scholar
49 posts
Only drawback is that its heavier than banded. It has an awesome look though. Fashion or function I guess.

BTW My smith on The Rathe can make this stuff all day long, and can't give it away. Ok, she can give it away, but sell it? Not even for 1pp per AC. Not even at COST. I could make these at 10pp each, and make a small profit, and a new player would have a great piece of armor, but apparently they don't need it.

Good thing I took up smithing and tailoring for my own uses. Smithing market, at least at low end, is dead.

Kill for Peace,
Elessarion: Fletcher, Baker, Brewer, Fisher, Forager
Kzinnia: Smith, Tailor
The Rathe
▲ Page top
RE: Loda Kai

I think the reason no one wnats to buy banded is because it isn't that much better than the newbie quest armor. And the newbie quest armor gives you stats to boot.
